scenes.script_line: OpenAI just shut down Sora, their most hyped product ever. Here is why.

scenes.visual_prompt: A glowing AI neural network being unplugged, wires disconnecting, dark futuristic server room, neon blue and red lighting
scenes.script_line: Sora was supposed to be the future of video generation. They teased it for over a year.

scenes.visual_prompt: robotics lab with robot arms moving
scenes.script_line: But running Sora was burning through compute like nothing else. We are talking billions of dollars in GPU costs.
The real reason? OpenAI is pivoting hard into robotics. And robots need that compute way more than video generation does.

scenes.visual_prompt: Humanoid robot walking through a modern office, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, slight lens flare
scenes.script_line: Sam Altman bet that physical AI is worth more than digital content creation.

scenes.visual_prompt: futuristic city skyline at night with AI holographic displays
scenes.script_line: Whether he is right or not, one thing is clear. The AI video generation race just lost its biggest player.
